creator: glarkin@FreeBSD.org
text:
The MOD_DONTDOTHAT option in the Makefile doesn't appear to control the build of mod_dontdothat with the following other options selected:

# This file is auto-generated by 'make config'.
# No user-servicable parts inside!
# Options for subversion-1.7.4_1
_OPTIONS_READ=subversion-1.7.4_1
WITH_P4_STYLE_MARKERS=true
WITH_ENHANCED_KEYWORD=true
WITHOUT_FREEBSD_TEMPLATE=true
WITH_MOD_DAV_SVN=true
WITHOUT_MOD_DONTDOTHAT=true
WITH_NEON=true
WITHOUT_SERF=true
WITHOUT_SASL=true
WITHOUT_BDB=true
WITHOUT_MAINTAINER_DEBUG=true
WITH_SVNSERVE_WRAPPER=true
WITH_SVNAUTHZ_VALIDATE=true
WITHOUT_STATIC=true
WITHOUT_GNOME_KEYRING=true
WITHOUT_KDE_KWALLET=true
WITHOUT_BOOK=true

I built subversion under tinderbox and the following log file was generated:
http://people.freebsd.org/~glarkin/logs/subversion-1.7.4_1.log

Fix: 

Perhaps the build-outputs.mk file needs to be conditionalized somehow?  The version in files/ is different than the one in the upstream dist now, in case that matters.
How-To-Repeat: Select the options noted above and attempt a build, install, deinstall of Subversion.
